docs.title: Vision
docs.text: There exists a future in which humans, augmented and assisted by intelligent machines, illuminate the galaxy and beyond with the light of consciousness. This future is not inevitable, and its realization depends on the choices we make today. Titor Technologies was founded to explore emerging tech and make strategic investments that keep humanity on an optimal timeline. We amplify this effect by sharing our research and teaching our methods to others who share our vision for a bright future.

docs.title: Investment Thesis
docs.text:
The idea is simple:

  • We use modern tools to forecast what technologies will exist in the futures we want to see.
  • We measure the gaps between the technologies of that future and current technologies.
  • We invest in existing undervalued technologies destined to grow and fill those gaps.

docs.title: Leadership cadence
docs.text:
• John Titor — CEO: charts the happy path and keeps our bets aligned with the timeline we’re intent on realizing.
  • Dean Pierce — CTO: tests every claim for clarity, coherence, and technical rigor so the strategy holds together.

docs.title: Analysis
docs.text: Research notes and deep-dives.

docs.title: Why it matters (for the happy path)
docs.text:
• Hard money as a coordination primitive (especially when institutions erode)
  • Censorship resistance, with real tradeoffs (privacy, UX, governance)

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• What L2s and custody models actually work for normal humans?
  • Where do mining/energy dynamics intersect with grid resilience?
  • What does "Bitcoin as settlement" look like when agents transact?

docs.title: EigenLayer
docs.text: Restaking is an attempt to turn Ethereum security into a modular commodity.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
• Security reuse can accelerate new networks/services
  • It can also create correlated risk (shared failure modes)

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• Where does restaking create real value vs. leverage loops?
  • What are the systemic-risk failure scenarios?
  • How do we get transparent, auditable risk disclosure?

docs.title: Ethereum
docs.text: Ethereum is the most mature programmable settlement layer for building credibly-neutral coordination mechanisms.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
• Smart contracts as institutional building blocks
  • Account abstraction + smart wallets → better human UX
  • L2s as the scaling path (with fragmentation risk)

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• Which primitives increase alignment bandwidth (identity, reputation, attestations)?
  • What reduces fraud/MEV-driven extraction?
  • What’s the practical path to privacy (ZK) without breaking usability?

docs.title: LayerZero
docs.text: Interoperability is either the bridge to composability—or the bridge to contagion.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
• Cross-chain messaging is a coordination force multiplier
  • It’s also historically a top source of catastrophic exploits

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• What trust assumptions are actually being made?
  • How do audits + verification scale?
  • Can we design “blast-radius-limited” messaging?

docs.title: Solana
docs.text: High-throughput, low-latency execution is valuable—but social + economic security is the long game.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
• Cheap, fast markets enable new coordination experiments
  • Consumer-grade UX pressure is real

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• Where is Solana genuinely the best tool vs. hype?
  • How does validator economics affect long-run decentralization?
  • What parts of the ecosystem are durable vs. incentive artifacts?

docs.title: THORChain
docs.text: Permissionless liquidity across chains is powerful.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
• Native-asset swaps reduce custodial choke points
  • DeFi infrastructure becomes critical plumbing in stressed environments

docs.title: Questions we track
docs.text:
• Security track record and upgrade governance
  • Real-world usage vs. mercenary liquidity
  • How resilient is it under adversarial conditions?

docs.title: Blog
docs.text: Short, high-signal updates from Titor Technologies: theses, research notes, and field reports.

docs.title: MKRBOX and the case for open fabrication
docs.text:
Open-source software won because it made progress composable: anyone could inspect, fork, improve, and deploy. But we haven’t done the same thing for manufacturing—especially the messy middle where prototypes become reliable, repeatable physical systems.

That gap is more than an economic inefficiency. It’s an alignment problem.

When the ability to manufacture tools, parts, and infrastructure is concentrated, the “happy path” for humanity becomes fragile: it depends on a small number of organizations getting incentives right, indefinitely. Open fabrication is a way to widen the set of actors who can build and maintain the physical world.

docs.title: Open manufacturing is woefully underinvested
docs.text:
We invest heavily in: - better models - better chips - better cloud - better devtools

Meanwhile the pipeline that turns knowledge into atoms—fixtures, fasteners, test rigs, repair parts, lab gear—remains fragmented and under-automated.

The result: progress is bottlenecked by a handful of vendor ecosystems and opaque supply chains, and the “build capacity” of communities is far below what’s possible.

docs.title: MKRBOX: a sim-first workcell for real-world build capacity
docs.text:
MKRBOX is a modular 1 m²-footprint workcell with roughly a 1 m³ kinematic reach envelope.

The design goal is not “a humanoid robot that does everything.” It’s something more pragmatic:

  • A tool-changing box with standardized bays and interfaces.
  • A sim-first pipeline (levels, replay, failure analysis) before touching hardware.
  • An operator-collaborative loop: the system requests modules/parts; a human supplies them; the box executes and verifies.

That operator-request pattern matters. It keeps the system productive without requiring full autonomy, and it keeps humans in the loop where it actually counts: materials, safety, and intent.

docs.title: Why this is alignment-critical
docs.text:
Alignment is not just about what models say.

It’s about what systems can do, at scale, and who controls that capability.

If advanced automation arrives before broad, open, inspectable build capacity, we risk a world where: - communities can’t repair or reproduce critical infrastructure - knowledge is abundant but physical capability is scarce - power concentrates around proprietary supply chains and closed tool stacks

Open fabrication pushes in the other direction: distributed competence, resilient local capacity, and tooling that can be audited and improved.

docs.title: What we’re building toward
docs.text:
MKRBOX is a pathway to a future where: - simulation levels become real-world build recipes - failure modes are cataloged and recoveries are automated - module standards let ecosystems emerge (tooling libraries, sensor packs, test fixtures)

The near-term milestone is simple: make the simulator fun and useful.

If we can turn manufacturing into a game—progression, failures, repair, scoring—we get the missing substrate: shared benchmarks and shared mechanics that map directly to real hardware.

docs.title: How to participate
docs.text:
Start with the simulator and the docs: - Define the box: https://mkrbox.org/SPEC.md - Shape the levels: https://mkrbox.org/LEVELS.md - Build the sim: https://mkrbox.org/SIM.md

If you care about the long-term “happy path,” open fabrication is not optional. It’s the physical layer of alignment.

docs.title: Welcome to the Titor blog
docs.text:
We’re starting a lightweight blog to publish weekly notes: what changed in the world, what it implies, and what we’re building.

Format will be short and decision-oriented: signals, forecasts, and action items.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text: Short, reusable building blocks for the Titor thesis. These are “modules” you can remix into strategies, product bets, or research directions.

docs.title: Index
docs.text:
• Infrastructure
  • Sustainability
  • Gamification
  • Lindy
  • Cypherpunk
  • Mechanical pantheon

If the left nav feels thin, this page is the hub that should make it feel like a tree again.

docs.title: Cypherpunk Ethos
docs.text:
Cypherpunk is the simple belief that privacy and freedom are engineering problems.

In the agent era, this becomes more urgent: agents will handle messages, money, identity, and automation. If those rails aren’t private and verifiable, power concentrates.

docs.title: Key primitives
docs.text:
• End-to-end encryption you can audit
  • Self-custody + smart wallets (with human-usable recovery)
  • Verifiable identity without mass surveillance
  • Credible neutrality at the protocol layer

docs.title: The happy-path framing
docs.text: We’re not building privacy to hide wrongdoing. We’re building it so ordinary people can coordinate, dissent, and live without being permanently scored.

docs.title: Gamification
docs.text:
In the happy path future of automation, we envision a world where most people are fully independent educators, builders, mechanics, or explorers. They work when and where they feel is best to serve their community. However, there will still likely be tasks that require large-scale coordination efforts. These tasks will be performed by the gamers.

Early-stage games will be designed for a younger audience to teach them the specifics of the industry they're operating in. As the game progresses, things become more "real" until their gameplay is directly training or operating a remote fleet of coordinated worker drones.

A number of Titor Technologies projects, including tinyfarm, autofac, moerc, art3mis, and ecology.click, explore different angles of this vision.

docs.title: Automated Tech Trees
docs.text: A large part of this vision is systems for a collaborative tech tree build-out. We think that we can build economic mechanisms that enable drift toward increasingly realistic systems to build the large-scale structures envisioned by these projects.

docs.title: Community-Owned Infrastructure
docs.text: A “happy path” future needs communities that can operate the basics without begging permission from distant monopolies.

docs.title: What counts as infrastructure
docs.text:
• Energy generation + storage (and the expertise to maintain it)
  • Connectivity (fiber, mesh, satellite, local networking)
  • Water, sanitation, shelter
  • Local fabrication + repair capacity (tools, parts, test rigs)
  • Compute (on-device, local clusters, and accountable cloud)

docs.title: The point
docs.text: Infrastructure isn’t just “utilities” — it’s agency. If you can’t keep the lights on, you can’t keep your institutions coherent.

docs.title: Investment primitives
docs.text:
• Open standards + interoperable hardware
  • Maintenance-first design (Lindy: boring wins)
  • Local operator toolchains (docs, training, spare parts)
  • Auditable automation (robots/agents that are inspectable and safe-by-default)

docs.title: Lindy
docs.text:
If something has survived a long time, it has (usually) discovered how not to die.

In a world of fast-moving AI capabilities, Lindy is a counterweight: prefer systems that degrade gracefully and can be repaired by normal humans.

docs.title: Lindy heuristics
docs.text:
• Prefer protocols over platforms
  • Prefer open formats over proprietary state
  • Prefer redundancy over cleverness
  • Prefer tools with decades of operational knowledge

docs.title: Caveat
docs.text: Lindy is not “old = good.” It’s “survival pressure created information.” Use it as a filter, not a religion.

docs.title: Mechanical Pantheon
docs.text:
A way to think about “AI systems” as a set of roles rather than a single god-model.

Instead of one monolith, imagine a pantheon of specialized, accountable agents: - Sentinel: watches for anomalies + abuse - Archivist: preserves memory + provenance - Operator: executes playbooks safely - Mediator: helps humans resolve conflicts - Builder: ships tools and infrastructure

docs.title: Why this matters
docs.text:
Specialization + clear interfaces makes systems: - easier to audit - easier to replace - less likely to fail catastrophically

It’s a governance idea disguised as a metaphor.

docs.title: Economic Sustainability
docs.text: “Sustainability” here is not vibes. It’s whether a system can keep functioning when: - demand spikes - supply chains break - politics turns hostile - your best people leave

docs.title: The core question
docs.text: What is the minimal, steady-state funding + maintenance loop that keeps a capability alive?

docs.title: What to look for
docs.text:
• Products/services where maintenance is naturally funded (not an afterthought)
  • Incentives aligned with uptime + safety (not growth at any cost)
  • Tooling that reduces operational burden (automation that decreases fragility)

docs.title: Why it matters for AI
docs.text: Agent systems that depend on a brittle funding model (or hidden subsidies) will fail exactly when we need them most.

docs.title: Forecasts
docs.text: Long-horizon theses and scenarios.

docs.title: 2030 — AGI Era
docs.text: A plausible near-term world where general-purpose models are strong enough to automate large fractions of knowledge work.

docs.title: Happy-path focus
docs.text:
• Alignment bandwidth increases (tools, interfaces, verification)
  • Education and retraining are treated as infrastructure
  • Energy + compute become explicit policy domains

docs.title: Boundary markers (20%)
docs.text:
• Mass persuasion + automated fraud becomes industrial
  • “Safety theater” replaces real verification

docs.title: What we should build now
docs.text:
• Agent supply-chain security (signed skills, permission manifests)
  • Human-usable cryptography + identity
  • Local resilience: repair, fabrication, and community ops

docs.title: 2040 — ASI Era
docs.text: A scenario where systems are not just “smart,” but operate with sustained strategic advantage in many domains.

docs.title: Happy-path focus
docs.text:
• Institutions become more legible (auditable, explainable governance)
  • Rights and agency are protected by infrastructure, not vibes
  • Abundance is real enough to defang zero-sum politics

docs.title: Boundary markers
docs.text:
• Closed-source sovereign ASI arms race
  • Human autonomy reduced to managed consumption

docs.title: What to watch
docs.text:
• Compute/energy concentration trends
  • Governance experiments that scale without coercion
  • The emergence (or absence) of verifiable safety layers

docs.title: 2045 — Singularity
docs.text: “Singularity” here means the future becomes hard to predict because the rate of capability improvement is too high.

docs.title: Happy-path framing
docs.text: The goal isn’t to control everything. It’s to ensure: - humans retain agency and dignity - systems remain auditable and corrigible - power doesn’t collapse into a single irreversible attractor

docs.title: Boundary markers
docs.text:
• One-way doors everywhere (irreversible deployments)
  • Value lock-in by narrow coalitions

docs.title: Strategic posture
docs.text:
• Build reversible systems
  • Prefer modularity + replaceability
  • Invest in tools that widen participation in building the physical world

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: A living index of the things we’re building (or monitoring) to widen the “happy path” for humans + machines.

docs.title: Active / tracked
docs.text:
• NWEB — Networked web primitives.
  • LNDY — Lindy-adjacent systems and design patterns.
  • W3RK — Work, tooling, and operational leverage.
  • Bastet — Security + resilience.
  • Storm Dance — Forecasts and scenario work.

If you’re looking for something specific and it’s missing, that’s a gap we should fill.

docs.title: bastet.ai
docs.text: An experimental infosec DAO and autonomous AI agent platform designed as a protective entity for organizations and communities. Originally conceived as a "silly idea for infosec dao," Bastet has evolved into a sophisticated system where users contribute USDC to community treasuries, which are staked in Compound v3 on Polygon for yield generation. Contributors receive NFT crystals granting access to protected spaces, while active supporters receive "blessings" (rewards) from the treasury yield.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Treasury Management: USDC contributions staked in Compound v3 on Polygon
  • NFT Access Tokens: Crystals granting access to protected community spaces
  • Yield Distribution: "Blessings" paid from treasury interest to contributors
  • Autonomous Agent: AI-powered community protection and management
  • Discord Integration: Community spaces and communication channels
  • OpenSea Collection: Tradeable NFT crystals with utility

docs.title: The Vision of Bastet
docs.text:
From the project's about page:

"The ultimate goal of this project is to summon Bastet, in her entirety, as a fully autonomous agent capable of protecting her domain."

The platform represents an experiment in:

  • Autonomous Community Management: AI agents managing human organizations
  • Decentralized Governance: Transition from manual to AI-driven decision making
  • Sustainable Funding: Treasury yield supporting community operations
  • Post-Corporate Organization: New models for human coordination

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Smart Contracts: Solidity on Polygon
  • Treasury: Compound v3 USDC staking (~5% APY)
  • NFTs: ERC-721 crystal tokens
  • Frontend: Web-based interface
  • AI Integration: OpenAI (transitioning to web3 AI platforms)
  • License: Apache License 2.0

docs.title: Current Implementation
docs.text:
• Treasury Staking: USDC deposited and earning yield in Compound v3
  • Reward Distribution: Interest pays "blessings" to active contributors
  • Access Control: NFT crystals grant Discord server access
  • Governance Evolution: Planned transition from multisig to DAO to full AI control

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Website: bastet.ai
  • GitHub: GitHub Repository
  • Discord: Temple of Bastet
  • OpenSea: Bastet AI Collection

docs.title: Future Development
docs.text: The project has multiple related repositories: - bastet: Core smart contracts and platform - bastet-meander: Research architecture - eliza-starter-bastet: Integration with Eliza AI framework

docs.title: Alignment with Titor Mission
docs.text: Bastet represents a critical experiment in beneficial AI development and autonomous community management. As Titor Technologies works toward a future where AI systems augment human capability, Bastet explores how autonomous agents can protect and nurture human communities. This project directly addresses the challenge of creating AI systems that serve human interests while operating independently, contributing to our vision of AI-assisted coordination that enhances rather than replaces human agency.

docs.title: lndy.org
docs.text: A social lending platform powered by Ethereum that enables peer-to-peer lending with smart contract automation. LNDY (a proof-of-concept for social lending protocol) allows users to create, fund, and manage loans in a decentralized manner, removing traditional financial intermediaries while maintaining security through blockchain technology.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Peer-to-Peer Lending: Direct lending between individuals
  • Smart Contract Automation: Ethereum-based loan management
  • Social Trust Networks: Lending based on personal relationships
  • Decentralized Finance: No traditional banking intermediaries
  • Transparent Terms: All loan terms visible on-chain
  • Automated Repayment: Smart contract-enforced payment schedules

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Blockchain: Ethereum
  • Smart Contracts: Solidity-based lending protocols
  • Frontend: TypeScript, React
  • Web3 Integration: Ethereum wallet connectivity

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
⚠️ Alpha Software Warning

LNDY is experimental software in early development. The platform includes important safety warnings:

  • Only invest money you can afford to lose completely
  • Only fund loans from people you know personally and trust
  • Borrowers may be unable or unwilling to repay for various reasons
  • Smart contracts may contain bugs
  • Use at your own risk

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Personal Loans: Between friends and family members
  • Community Lending: Within trusted social circles
  • Microfinance: Small-scale lending for entrepreneurial ventures
  • Emergency Funds: Quick access to capital from trusted networks

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Website: lndy.org
  • GitHub: GitHub Repository

docs.title: Alignment with Titor Mission
docs.text: LNDY represents an experiment in post-traditional financial systems, demonstrating how blockchain technology can enable new forms of economic coordination based on social trust rather than institutional authority. This project explores the future of finance in a world where traditional banking infrastructure may be supplemented or replaced by decentralized protocols that better serve human needs and relationships.

docs.title: nweb.io
docs.text: A web-based Nmap scan collection and search platform that aggregates and displays network security reconnaissance data. nweb.io provides comprehensive network analysis capabilities, showing detailed scan results including open ports, service detection, and security assessments across internet-facing systems. This tool serves security researchers and organizations seeking to understand network exposure and vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Nmap Scan Aggregation: Collection and storage of network scan data
  • Service Detection: Detailed analysis of running services and versions
  • Port Analysis: Comprehensive open port reporting
  • Search Interface: Easy searching through scan results
  • Vulnerability Assessment: Security exposure analysis
  • Real-time Data: Current network reconnaissance information

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Frontend: CSS, HTML
  • Backend: Web-based interface
  • Data Source: Nmap scan results
  • Search: Custom search and filtering capabilities

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Security Research: Network vulnerability discovery
  • Penetration Testing: Reconnaissance phase support
  • Asset Discovery: Understanding network infrastructure
  • Compliance Auditing: Security posture assessment
  • Threat Intelligence: Understanding attack surfaces

docs.title: Data Types
docs.text:
The platform displays various types of network reconnaissance data:

  • IP Address Analysis: Host information and geolocation
  • Open Ports: Service ports and their states
  • Service Fingerprinting: Software versions and configurations
  • SSL Certificate Analysis: Certificate validity and chains
  • Operating System Detection: Host OS identification
  • Network Topology: Infrastructure mapping

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Website: nweb.io
  • GitHub: GitHub Repository

docs.title: Alignment with Titor Mission
docs.text: nweb.io provides essential cybersecurity intelligence capabilities needed to protect the infrastructure supporting emerging technologies. As Titor Technologies invests in decentralized systems and autonomous agents, robust security assessment tools become critical for identifying vulnerabilities and protecting against threats that could undermine technological progress toward beneficial futures.

docs.title: storm.dance
docs.text: A decentralized note-taking application that enables users to create, edit, and store notes using IPFS for distributed storage and XMTP for encrypted messaging. storm.dance represents the future of knowledge management by removing dependence on centralized platforms, allowing for truly decentralized content creation and collaboration.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Decentralized Storage: Uses IPFS for distributed note storage
  • Encrypted Messaging: XMTP integration for secure communication
  • Web3 Integration: MetaMask wallet connection for identity management
  • Collaborative Notebooks: Create and share notebook collections
  • No Central Authority: Fully decentralized with no single point of failure

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Frontend: TypeScript, React
  • Storage: IPFS (InterPlanetary File System)
  • Messaging: XMTP (Extensible Message Transport Protocol)
  • Web3: MetaMask integration, Ethereum wallet support
  • License: Apache License 2.0

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Website: storm.dance
  • GitHub: GitHub Repository

docs.title: Alignment with Titor Mission
docs.text: storm.dance demonstrates how decentralized technologies can replace traditional centralized platforms, creating resilient systems that empower users while reducing dependence on corporate infrastructure. This project represents our vision of a future where knowledge and communication remain under user control, supporting the development of truly autonomous systems that serve humanity's interests.

docs.title: w3rk.net
docs.text: A web3 jobs platform designed to provide Universal Basic Income (UBI) and Universal Economic Incentive (UEI) payments for job seekers. Built as a super-simple MVP shell that's production-deployable on Vercel, w3rk.net is ready to evolve into a comprehensive mini-app featuring escrow functionality, XMTP chat integration, and an Active Seeker Dividend (ASD) system.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• UBI/UEI Payments: Weekly payments for active job seekers
  • On-chain Escrow: Smart contract-based payment protection
  • XMTP Chat: Encrypted messaging between employers and workers
  • Active Seeker Dividend: Rewards for platform participation
  • Base Blockchain: Built on the Base network for low fees and high throughput
  • Vercel Deployment: Zero-config production deployment

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js App Router
  • Blockchain: Base (Ethereum L2)
  • Messaging: XMTP integration
  • Deployment: Vercel-ready with zero server configuration
  • Framework: Next.js with App Router pattern

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
The platform is currently in MVP stage as a production-deployable shell, ready to evolve into a full-featured application with:

  • Smart contract escrow systems
  • Comprehensive job matching algorithms
  • Advanced UBI distribution mechanisms
  • Community governance features

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Website: w3rk.net
  • GitHub: GitHub Repository

docs.title: Alignment with Titor Mission
docs.text: w3rk.net addresses the fundamental transition from traditional employment models to decentralized work coordination. By implementing UBI-ready features and post-scarcity economic models, this platform prepares for a future where human labor is augmented and partially replaced by AI systems, ensuring that the benefits of technological advancement are distributed equitably across society.
